docs.title: GPU Inference with Podman
docs.text: Run AI inference inside a Linux container on macOS using the Apple GPU — powered by LibKrun's Virtio-GPU bridge between Vulkan and Metal.

docs.title: Create a GPU-Enabled Machine
docs.text:
In Podman Desktop, go to Settings → Resources → Podman.

Click Create new and set the Machine provider to libkrun (GPU enabled). Set CPU and memory as needed, then click Create.

Start the machine once created.

Note: You need to delete any existing Podman machine first to avoid port conflicts. LibKrun replaces the default Apple Hypervisor provider.

docs.title: Verify GPU is Available
docs.text:
podman machine ssh
ls /dev/dri

You should see card0 and renderD128. If both are present the GPU is accessible inside the VM.

Exit the SSH session:

exit

docs.title: Run Inference
docs.text:
podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri \
  -v ~/models:/models:Z \
  quay.io/slopezpa/fedora-vgpu-llama

--device /dev/dri passes the GPU into the container. -v ~/models:/models:Z mounts a local folder where you store your model files.

Inside the container, run inference against a GGUF model:

main -m /models/your-model.gguf -ngl 99 -p "Your prompt here"

-ngl 99 offloads all layers to the GPU. Adjust down if you run out of VRAM.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
Already done this before?

  1. Open Podman Desktop → Settings → Resources → Podman → Create new → select libkrun (GPU enabled) → Create → Start.
  2. Run:
podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri \
  -v ~/models:/models:Z \
  quay.io/slopezpa/fedora-vgpu-llama

docs.title: Podman Desktop + Freelens
docs.text: Podman Desktop manages containers and local Kubernetes clusters. Freelens is a free, open-source GUI for browsing and managing those clusters — no CLI needed.

docs.title: Install Tools
docs.text:
brew install --cask podman-desktop
brew install helm
brew install kind
brew install kubernetes-cli
brew install --cask freelens

docs.title: 1. Launch and Create a Machine
docs.text:
Open Finder (⌃ + ⌥ + F), go to Applications, and launch Podman Desktop.

On first launch the setup wizard appears. Click Install when prompted — this creates and starts your Podman machine automatically.

docs.title: 2. Install the Kind Extension
docs.text:
1. Click Extensions in the left sidebar.
  2. Search for Kind.
  3. Click the install icon next to it.
  4. Go to the Installed tab and confirm it is active.

docs.title: 3. Create a Kubernetes Cluster
docs.text:
1. Click Kubernetes in the left sidebar.
  2. Click Create new cluster.
  3. Select Kind as the provider.
  4. Give it a name (e.g. dev-cluster) and click Create.

Podman Desktop will spin up the cluster and set it as your active context.

docs.title: 1. Launch Freelens
docs.text: Open Freelens from Applications. It automatically reads your kubeconfig and detects dev-cluster.

docs.title: 2. Browse the Cluster
docs.text: Click on dev-cluster in the sidebar to connect. From here you can browse Workloads, Deployments, Pods, Services, and more — all without touching the terminal.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
Already done this before? Just reinstall the tools.

brew install --cask podman-desktop
brew install helm
brew install kind
brew install kubernetes-cli
brew install --cask freelens

Then launch Podman Desktop to recreate the machine and cluster, and Freelens to connect.
